# APA reporting style for statistical tests {#sec-a1report}

## Correlation (Pearson's $r$)

r(<b>degrees of freedom</b>) = <b>correlation coefficient</b> p = <b>p-value</b>

## t-tests {#sec-a1t}

t(<b>degrees of freedom</b>) = <b>t-value</b>,  p = <b>p-value</b>

## ANOVA {#sec-a1anova}

F(<b>numerator/effect degrees of freedom</b>,  <b>denominator/residual degrees of freedom</b>) = <b>F-value</b>,  p = <b>p-value</b>, 

## $\chi^2$ {#sec-a1chi}

$\chi^2$(_n_, <b>degrees of freedom</b>) = <b>chi-squared value</b>,  p = <b>p-value</b>

## Regression {#sec-a1reg}

* Omnibus F-test of the model should be reported as for ANOVA (see above)
* Coefficients: b = <b>coefficient/slope value</b>, t(<b>degrees of freedom</b>) = <b>t-value</b>,  p = <b>p-value</b>
